description: Game Development — design, prototyping, balancing, level design, playtesting, and asset integration. Commander: ARDENT.

model.notes: Tier 1 for routine design tasks. Tier 2 for deep mechanic prototyping and balance analysis. Cross-division: reads production packets for asset status.

game-design.notes: Iterates on game design document sections. Reads production art-director packets for visual direction.

mechanic-prototype.notes: Generates pseudocode and logic specs for game mechanics.

balance-audit.notes: Analyzes game data for balance issues. Reads playtest-report packets.

level-design.notes: Procedural level layout generation and iteration.

tech-spec.notes: Generates technical design documents for game systems.

playtest-report.notes: Analyzes playtest data, flags issues, feeds back to balance-audit and production qa-pipeline.

asset-integration.notes: Cross-division: reads production asset-catalog and asset-deliver packets. Flags gaps against game design requirements.

gamedev-digest.notes: Daily synthesis of all game dev activity. Reads all gamedev skill packets.

code-generate.notes: Generates game source code from tech specs and GDD. Uses Coder 14B for higher quality. Supports godot, pygame, generic targets.

code-review.notes: Reviews generated game code for bugs, architecture issues, and best practices. Uses Coder 14B.

code-test.notes: Generates and runs test suites for game code. LLM for generation, deterministic for execution.

build-pipeline.notes: Packages game projects for distribution. Pure Python — no LLM needed.

scene-assemble.notes: Wires Production's delivered assets into game scenes. Reads level-design specs and production packets.

character-designer.notes: Designs player and NPC characters with stats, backstory, visual identity, and class archetypes.

item-forge.notes: Creates game items — weapons, armor, consumables — with stats, rarity tiers, and lore.

enemy-designer.notes: Designs enemies and bosses with AI behaviors, attack patterns, phases, and loot tables.

quest-writer.notes: Writes quest definitions — objectives, branching dialogue, rewards, and narrative hooks.

story-writer.notes: Writes game narrative — acts, chapters, world lore, and character arcs.

skill-tree-builder.notes: Designs class skill trees with abilities, prerequisites, and balance curves.

asset-requester.notes: Generates asset request manifests for Production division. Schedule: after content design runs.

project-init.notes: Run once to initialize project structure. Scaffolds Godot/Pygame project with standard layout.

iteration-runner.notes: Automated code iteration loop. Generate, review, fix, test — repeats until code passes.

data-populate.notes: Run after content design to generate balance data. Populates item tables, enemy stats, XP curves.

game-factory.notes: Master pipeline — chains all gamedev skills autonomously. Runs weekly Saturday 04:00.

production-bridge.notes: Cross-division wiring — bridges gamedev specs to production asset pipeline.

game-runner.notes: Launches and executes game builds for testing. Supports pygame and godot targets.

auto-playtest.notes: Automated playtesting — runs game scenarios, captures metrics, flags issues.

visual-qa.notes: Visual quality assurance — inspects game screenshots, sprites, and scene renders.

refine-loop.notes: Iterative refinement pipeline — cycles generate/test/fix until quality passes.
